refinement_sections.content: We need to figure out if our new product is selling well compared to a specific sales target. This means we have to compare the average sales from the last three months to this target.

We should state two hypotheses. The null hypothesis (H0) could be that the average sales are less than or equal to the benchmark. The alternative hypothesis (H1) would be that the average sales are greater than the benchmark.
We will use the sales data collected by our sales team from the last three months. We need to make sure we have all the sales figures for this period.
We should choose a statistical test to use. A t-test might be appropriate if we assume that the sales data is normally distributed.
We need to mention any assumptions we make, like if the sales data follows a normal distribution or if the sample size is large enough.

Finally, we will write a report that explains how we did the test, what the results were, and if we think the product is doing well or not. We need to make sure to include our assumptions and how they might change our conclusions.
